page: Amazon Web Services t2.xlarge is a burstable performance instance belonging to the t2 family, running on the Xen hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs, with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate that the t2.xlarge delivers modest performance, with many metrics falling into the lower performance categories. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 881.6 is in the bottom 10%, while the multi-core score of 3507.84 is in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show read-only large block speeds of 25.72 GB/sec and write speeds of 17.13 GB/sec, both in the bottom 25%. Similarly, database operations and Redis SET operations (2.02 million operations/sec) rank in the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. However, the instance achieves an average performance tier in small-model text generation (135M model) at 122.66 tokens/sec.

Given its burstable CPU allocation and resource density, the t2.xlarge offers a cost-effective profile for workloads with variable CPU utilization. Because it relies on a CPU credit system to burst above baseline performance, it is not suited for sustained high-compute tasks, as reflected in its lower multi-core and single-core benchmark rankings. This instance is best suited for low-traffic web serving, development and testing environments, code compilation, and light database operations that experience periodic spikes in activity rather than continuous heavy loads.
description: Amazon Web Services t2.xlarge is a burstable general-purpose instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 16.0 GB of RAM on an x86_64 Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz. Operating under the Xen hypervisor, it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data positions this instance primarily in the lower performance tiers, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core scores in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively, and a Redis SET throughput of 2.02 million operations per second. However, it maintains average performance for small-model LLM text generation. Due to its burstable CPU allocation, the t2.xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads with fluctuating CPU demands, making it suitable for development environments, light database tasks, and low-traffic web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t2.xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 CPU, offering cost-effective performance for workloads with variable resource demands.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t2.xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, suited for variable workloads.
tagline: A burstable general-purpose instance offering balanced memory and variable CPU performance for development and light, non-sustained workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor with 4 vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM.
Utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model suitable for variable workloads.
Delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps with no local storage.
Performs in the lower tiers for sustained compute and database operations.
Best suited for development environments, code compilation, and low-traffic web serving.
